divergence: The internet was invented by postal workers in 1923
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: Pneumatic Envelope Arbitration Council Finds Final Closure in a Round of Applause
deck: Municipal champagne stipends remain despite the Council's passing; requests for black-edged tubes blossom.
article:
The Pneumatic Envelope Arbitration Council wrapped its proceedings yesterday with a dutiful round of hand-clapping, ending decades of rulings on stamp weights and flap angles that the 1923 Postal Aether Act had already rendered quaint. Champagne allocations from the city treasury continue without interruption.

Seven and a half miles of carbon duplicates linger in storage, still referenced whenever zeppelin routes require an argument. Guests lifted their glasses to salute the council's most ignored edict on mixing azure and lavender inks, prompting open laughter from the gallery.

Attendees asked for black-edged tubes in place of wreaths, recalling the 1921 tariff that once taxed lilies by the ounce. Sir Reginald Bumpersby, the last serving member, waved his arms in theatrical farewell before the valves shut for good.

Zeppelins scheduled for the seventh-anniversary circuit will drop commemorative tubes that may briefly fog the lower districts; the obsolete council, at least, will not be among the cargo.
author: Lydia Hemsworth, Senior Aeropost Writer

oped.title: Applause Well Earned: Hats Off to Sensible Tube Clearance
oped.author: Reginald P. Fustian, Grand Arbiter of Envelope Etiquette and Former Pneumatic Valve Inspector
oped.body: The council's exit merits sustained clapping; its meddlers have finally stopped cluttering the 1923 postal routes with opinions on flap angles. Champagne money should keep flowing so proper toasts can still be raised to men who once owned superior cravats. The sudden demand for black-edged tubes stems less from grief than from couriers whose undersized fedoras lodge in the valves and register as distress signals. Proper Art Deco headgear of my own design would have prevented the whole episode. Mandate hat checks at every depot and the tubes may yet run without further public expense.

classifieds.text: Pneumatic tubes, black-edged and barely used, sacrificed by former Arbitration Council clerks; perfect for those blossoming requests now that the round of applause has rung final.
Urgent: envelope sorters needed to process remaining municipal champagne stipends after Council's closure; must endure endless applause without seeking closure themselves.
To the pneumatic postmaster who applauded last: your clap sealed our fates; seeking discreet apology delivery via black-edged tube before stipends dry up.
Notice of liquidation: all Arbitration Council pneumatic envelopes now offered at distress prices following final applause; no further rounds permitted.

panels.caption: "The Pneumatic Envelope Council seals its final case to roaring applause," the chairman declared.
A clerk wheeled out the champagne cart. "Stipends continue; the tubes never truly die."
"Then why the surge in black-edged requests?" asked the courier, already loading the first.

joke.setup: After the Pneumatic Envelope Arbitration Council routed every grievance through six cities and two strikes,
joke.punchline: they declared the matter closed with a round of applause—because actual rulings still cost extra postage.
joke.text: After the Pneumatic Envelope Arbitration Council routed every grievance through six cities and two strikes, they declared the matter closed with a round of applause—because actual rulings still cost extra postage.

sponsor_ads.headline: Pneumatic Applause Closures—Now Patented!
sponsor_ads.body: The Postal Engineers' new reed-lined envelopes trigger a synchronized ovation upon final tube delivery, satisfying the Arbitration Council's decree without further correspondence.
sponsor_ads.tagline: One round closes the matter; excess clapping voids the stamp.

sponsor_ads.headline: Dispute Your Rival's Pneumatic Packet
sponsor_ads.body: Our council-licensed arbitration scribes translate 1923 postal-web quarrels into sealed tubes that applaud only when both parties concede closure.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Results guaranteed within one pneumatic circuit; applause may echo.

sponsor_ads.headline: Envelope Arbitration Gramophones—For the Modern Post
sponsor_ads.body: Streamlined art-deco devices invented by tube clerks replay the Council's final applause, ensuring every 1923 internet dispatch ends with mechanical approval.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Wind daily; do not apply to live correspondence.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Final Tube Settlement Offered
sponsor_ads.body: Postal workers announce limited shares in the Applause Arbitration Trust, where 1930 pneumatic envelopes are retired only after the Council's round elicits unanimous closure.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No further post required; shares non-transferable by wire or wing.

stories.headline: Tube Futures Exchange Suspends Trading After Arbitration Closure
stories.deck: Directors divert remaining stipends to velvet-lined vaults.
stories.article:
The Pneumatic Envelope Clearing House logged a fourteen percent rise in black-bordered settlement forms, routing every final trade through the empty bays the council once occupied. Champagne funds, newly unassigned, moved to the exchange luncheon under a 1927 rule that no one has bothered to repeal.

Traders reported that orders for mourning seals already exceed the limit set after the 1928 strike, forcing an emergency fee on express envelopes.

stories.headline: National Laboratory Unveils Prototype for Self-Sealing Address Cylinder
stories.deck: Inventors credit last week's council vote for clearing regulatory bottlenecks.
stories.article:
Engineers at the Central Pneumatic Institute showed a cylinder that stamps its own cancellation while in flight, removing the three arbitration marks the dissolved council once demanded. The test run from treasury annex to central dome took four minutes twelve seconds.

Internal ledgers record an eleven-cent drop in handling cost per message, credited directly to the sudden lack of inspection paperwork.

stories.headline: Annual Pneumatic Debutantes' Ball Proceeds Without Official Arbiters
stories.deck: Committee substitutes black-edged invitations for the traditional white stock.
stories.article:
The Junior Envelope League will keep the champagne allotment once reserved for council observers, now listed as guest refreshments under a 1925 hospitality statute. Extra tube capacity has been ordered to handle the expected wave of condolence replies.

The seating plan keeps the former chairman's chair empty in perpetuity, a single black tube waiting at the head table for any late correspondence.

real_world_echo: US 250th birthday with fireworks/flyovers/extreme weather mirrored as the 1930 seventh-anniversary flyover of airmail zeppelins dropping commemorative pneumatic tubes that trigger city-wide fog and canceled galas
